Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class SystemRomProvider

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Provides Apple IIe system ROM storage and access.

public sealed class SystemRomProvider : ISystemRomProvider, IMemory
Inheritance
object
SystemRomProvider
Implements
ISystemRomProvider
IMemory
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Manages the 16KB system ROM ($C000-$FFFF) including I/O firmware, peripheral ROMs, Monitor ROM, and Applesoft BASIC. The ROM can be loaded from either a file or an embedded resource.

ROM Layout (16KB): 

$C000-$C0FF (256 bytes)  - I/O space firmware
$C100-$C7FF (1792 bytes) - Internal peripheral ROM (7 x 256 bytes)
$C800-$CFFF (2KB)        - Extended internal ROM
$D000-$DFFF (4KB)        - Monitor ROM / Language Card bank switching area
$E000-$FFFF (8KB)        - Applesoft BASIC + reset vector / Language Card common area

Loading Sources:

  • File System: Standard file path (e.g., "roms/AppleIIe.rom")
  • Embedded Resource: Use "res:" prefix (e.g., "res:Pandowdy.EmuCore.Resources.a2e_enh_c-f.rom")

Address Mapping: The ROM provider's address space starts at offset 0, representing $C000. To access $D000, use offset 0x1000. To access $FFFF, use offset 0x3FFF.

Constructors

SystemRomProvider(string)

Initializes a new instance of the SystemRomProvider class and loads ROM.

public SystemRomProvider(string filename)

Parameters

filename string

Path to the ROM file or resource identifier. Must be exactly 16KB (16,384 bytes). Use "res:" prefix for embedded resources (e.g., "res:Pandowdy.EmuCore.Resources.a2e_enh_c-f.rom").

Remarks

File System Example: 

var rom = new SystemRomProvider("roms/AppleIIe.rom");

Embedded Resource Example: 

var rom = new SystemRomProvider("res:Pandowdy.EmuCore.Resources.a2e_enh_c-f.rom");

Exceptions

ArgumentNullException

Thrown if filename is null.

FileNotFoundException

Thrown if the ROM file/resource cannot be found.

InvalidDataException

Thrown if the ROM is not exactly 16KB or cannot be read.

Properties

this[ushort]

Gets or sets a byte at the specified address. Setter is a no-op (ROM is read-only).

public byte this[ushort address] { get; set; }

Parameters

address ushort

Address within the ROM (0x0000-0x3FFF).

Property Value

byte

Byte value at the specified address.

Size

Gets the size of the ROM in bytes (always 16KB).

public int Size { get; }

Property Value

int

Methods

Read(ushort)

Reads a byte from the ROM at the specified address.

public byte Read(ushort address)

Parameters

address ushort

Address within the ROM (0x0000-0x3FFF representing $C000-$FFFF).

Returns

byte

Byte value at the specified address.

Remarks

Address Mapping:

  • 0x0000 = $C000 (I/O firmware start)
  • 0x1000 = $D000 (Monitor ROM start / Language Card bank area)
  • 0x2000 = $E000 (BASIC ROM start / Language Card common area)
  • 0x3FFF = $FFFF (End of ROM / Reset vector high byte)

Exceptions

IndexOutOfRangeException

Thrown if address is beyond ROM size.

Write(ushort, byte)

Write operation - ROM is read-only, this is a no-op.

public void Write(ushort address, byte data)

Parameters

address ushort

Address (ignored).

data byte

Data (ignored).

Remarks

ROM is read-only. Writes are silently ignored to maintain IMemory compatibility. In real hardware, ROM writes have no effect.
